_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 21:49:50Z)**_

Both changed regions are reviewed. This is a static React mockup (`docs/vercel_mockup/src/MOCKUP.jsx`) with hardcoded illustrative data — no backend, no real data sources, no privileged sinks.

Analysis of each change:

1. **`'all'` → `'multi'` literal + `vesselScale` math (lines 6409-6477)**: The KPI numbers are derived from hardcoded constants (`621900`, `1164`) multiplied by a numeric scale. Comment explicitly states "illustrative — visual feedback only". No real data filtering, no authorization decision — purely cosmetic numerics displayed to the viewer. The `effectiveVesselKey` is consumed only as a switch key for hardcoded occupancy percentages. No injection sink, no trust boundary.

2. **`readOnly` gating the port `<select>` (lines 6537-6555)**: This is a UI-only display change — the dropdown becomes a static label. Even with the dropdown present, `portFilter` only feeds the visual `filterScale` multiplier above; it does not gate real data. No authorization-by-UI concern, because the underlying numbers are constants in the bundle.

3. **`isActive` disambiguation in the nav (lines 18128-18155)**: `s.viewerSeed`, `s.href`, `s.label`, `s.id` are all literals defined inline in the same array. `window.open(s.href, '_blank')` opens a hardcoded internal path. `setCurrentUser(s.viewerSeed)` writes a hardcoded seed object to local React state. No attacker-influenceable input, no sink. React auto-escapes the rendered `s.label`.

No source→sink path with attacker influence exists in these diffs. No authorization control is being relaxed — `readOnly` is restricting (not loosening) UI affordance, and the underlying data is static.
